name: Web Reliability Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof that the reliability (error-recovery) runtime is conformant. The runtime (the customRecovery registry walking CustomRecoveryHandlers first-accept-wins) is relocated to Frontier UI (#1916, WE holds zero executable); its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788/#1790) driving the WE-owned reliability vector suite (#1919: deep-equal on the returned RecoveryResult / null — first-accept-wins, priority order, queued/abort outcomes, empty-registry no-recovery) against the FUI binding. The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside a cross-origin iframe.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof that the reliability (error-recovery) runtime is conformant. The runtime — the customRecovery registry walking CustomRecoveryHandlers first-accept-wins (#1051) — is relocated to Frontier UI (#1916; WE holds zero executable, #1282). Its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788 / #1790), which drives the WE reliability vector suite (#1919) against the FUI binding.

Verified contract

WE owns only the build-agnostic vectors and the type-only CustomRecoveryHandler contract (@webeverything/contracts/reliability); the handler-registry impl is FUI's, the runner is plateau's. The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside an iframe — the cross-origin boundary (#1788 ratified (b)) that reaches the shared, neutral runner with no implementer→runner edge. A green result proves conformance independent of how the runtime is built (#899).
